curation_notes: The advisory references commit a37f594 (test-only, added after the fix) and the v1.2.1...v1.2.2 release comparison. The actual security fix is commit 64dd950 ('fix idempotence'), which replaces direct charAt() lookups with new unescapedChar()/unescapedCharRev() helper methods in the sanitizeString method. This ensures escape sequences are properly decoded when checking for dangerous HTML/XML patterns (<script, <!--, -->, ]]>). The pre-patch commit (89d91f3, parent of 64dd950) contains a partial mitigation that checks for backslash characters but does not properly decode escape sequences. Only one non-test source file was modified: src/main/java/com/google/json/JsonSanitizer.java. The vulnerability class was pre-assigned as xxe (CWE-611); while the vulnerability mechanism is closer to output encoding bypass enabling HTML/XML injection, the CDATA section delimiter aspect relates to XML contexts consistent with the CWE assignment.
